name: Playwright
description: Cross-browser end-to-end testing framework from Microsoft — automates Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit with auto-waiting, network interception, and visual comparison
when_to_use: E2E testing of web applications, cross-browser compatibility testing, visual regression testing
what_it_finds: UI regressions, broken user flows, cross-browser inconsistencies, accessibility issues

name: Cypress
description: JavaScript end-to-end testing framework with time-travel debugging, automatic waiting, and real-time browser preview
when_to_use: E2E testing of web applications, component testing, testing complex user interactions with visual debugging
what_it_finds: UI regressions, broken user flows, flaky interactions, API integration issues

name: Selenium WebDriver
description: Browser automation framework for end-to-end testing — supports all major browsers via WebDriver protocol with bindings for multiple languages
when_to_use: Cross-browser E2E testing, legacy browser support, when you need language flexibility beyond JS/TS
what_it_finds: UI regressions, browser compatibility issues, broken workflows

install.command: pip install selenium || npm install selenium-webdriver

name: HTTPie
description: Human-friendly HTTP client for API testing — intuitive syntax for sending requests, inspecting responses, and validating JSON output
when_to_use: Manual API testing, exploring REST endpoints, debugging HTTP requests and responses

install.command: brew install httpie || pip install httpie

name: Hurl
description: Run and test HTTP requests defined in plain text files — chain requests, capture values, and assert responses with a simple declarative syntax
when_to_use: API integration testing, CI pipeline API validation, testing request chains with captured variables
what_it_finds: API contract violations, unexpected response codes, broken request chains, regression in API behavior

install.command: brew install hurl || cargo install hurl

name: Bruno
description: Git-friendly API client and testing tool — stores API collections as plain files in your repo, with scripting and assertions
when_to_use: API testing with version-controlled collections, replacing Postman/Insomnia with a git-native workflow
what_it_finds: API contract violations, broken endpoints, unexpected response shapes

name: k6
description: Modern load testing tool using JavaScript scripts — measures response times, throughput, and error rates under simulated traffic
when_to_use: Load testing APIs and websites, performance benchmarking, stress testing before releases
what_it_finds: Performance bottlenecks, response time degradation under load, error rate thresholds, throughput limits

install.command: brew install k6 || go install go.k6.io/k6@latest

name: Vegeta
description: HTTP load testing tool delivering constant request rates — pipe-based design for composable load test pipelines with histogram and latency reports
when_to_use: Constant-rate load testing, measuring latency distribution at fixed QPS, quick stress tests from CLI
what_it_finds: Latency percentiles under load, error rates, throughput ceilings, request timeout patterns

install.command: brew install vegeta || go install github.com/tsenart/vegeta@latest

name: hey
description: Simple HTTP load generator — quickly send N requests or sustain load for a duration and get a concise latency summary
when_to_use: Quick ad-hoc load tests, benchmarking a single endpoint, comparing response times before and after a change
what_it_finds: Response time distribution, requests per second, error percentages

install.command: brew install hey || go install github.com/rakyll/hey@latest

name: Stryker Mutator
description: Mutation testing framework — introduces small code mutations and checks if your tests catch them, measuring real test effectiveness
when_to_use: To validate test quality, find tests that pass even with bugs, measure mutation score beyond coverage
what_it_finds: Survived mutants (tests that pass despite code changes), weak assertions, untested logic branches

name: mutmut
description: Python mutation testing tool — modifies source code and runs your test suite to verify tests catch the mutations
when_to_use: To validate Python test quality, identify weak tests, measure mutation score
what_it_finds: Survived mutants, weak assertions, untested code paths, tests that pass regardless of code correctness

name: Locust
description: Scalable load testing tool written in Python — define user behavior in Python code with a web UI for real-time monitoring of test runs
when_to_use: Load testing with complex user scenarios defined in Python, distributed load generation, real-time monitoring via web UI
what_it_finds: Response time distribution, requests per second, failure rates, performance degradation curves under increasing load

name: Artillery
description: Cloud-scale load testing toolkit — test HTTP, WebSocket, Socket.io, and gRPC services with YAML scenarios and JavaScript hooks
when_to_use: Load testing APIs and microservices with complex scenarios, testing WebSocket and gRPC endpoints, cloud-distributed load generation
what_it_finds: Latency percentiles, error rates, throughput limits, protocol-specific failures, SLA violations

name: Pact
description: Consumer-driven contract testing framework — verifies that services can communicate by testing interactions at the boundary, not end-to-end
when_to_use: Microservice integration testing, verifying API contracts between consumer and provider without deploying both services together
what_it_finds: Breaking API changes, contract violations, incompatible request/response shapes between services

name: Testcontainers
description: Library for providing throwaway Docker containers for integration testing — spin up real databases, message brokers, and services in tests
when_to_use: Integration testing against real services (Postgres, Redis, Kafka, Elasticsearch) without mocking, ensuring tests match production behavior

install.command: npm install -D testcontainers || pip install testcontainers
